name: J.K. Dobbins
headline: Efficient runner, fragile body, touchdown-dependent ceiling
body:
Dobbins keeps producing yards the moment he touches the ball. Across 10 games in 2025 he ripped off 772 rushing yards on 153 carries at 5.05 per pop, with a 13.7% explosive-run rate and four scores before another foot injury ended his year at RB33. His 2.44 yards after contact per attempt and nine broken tackles show the burst is intact; the problem has never been talent. It has been staying on the field.

The concern is volume and pass-game floor. A 2.4% target share and 51.1% snap rate point to an early-down, committee role behind a passing-down complement, so his fantasy value leans heavily on finding the end zone. He flashed a near-neutral -0.005 EPA per play on a thin rushing context, and the 2024 line (905 yards, nine TDs, RB24) is the realistic upside. Treat him as a touchdown-reliant RB2 or flex with real injury risk baked in.
